UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user profile in the NexGenShop application, including personal information and assigned roles. Authentication details like passwords are managed externally.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: User's email address, used for OTP login and notifications. Must be unique if provided.

phoneNumber.description: User's phone number, used for OTP login and notifications. Must be unique if provided.

role.description: The role assigned to the user, defining their access permissions (e.g., 'user', 'admin', 'super_admin', 'support').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Address.title: Address
Address.description: Stores a physical address associated with a user, used for shipping and billing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Address entity.

street.description: Street address, including house number, building name, etc.

state.description: State, province, or region of the address.

zipCode.description: Postal code or ZIP code of the address.

isDefault.description: Indicates if this is the user's default address for its type.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Defines product categories to organize the product catalog.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the product category (e.g., 'Electronics', 'Apparel').

description.description: A brief description of the category.

slug.description: URL-friendly slug for the category, used for routing and SEO. Must be unique.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image representing the category.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a single product in the e-commerce catalog, excluding specific variants.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the product, optimized for SEO.

slug.description: URL-friendly slug for the product, used for routing and SEO. Must be unique.

price.description: The base price of the product.

brand.description: The brand name of the product.

images.description: An array of URLs for product images.

metadata.description: SEO metadata in JSON-LD or similar format, generated by AI tool.

averageRating.description: The average rating of the product based on user reviews.

reviewCount.description: The total number of reviews for the product.

isAvailable.description: Indicates if the product (any variant) is currently available for purchase.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product was added.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product details were last updated.

ProductVariant.title: ProductVariant
ProductVariant.description: Represents a specific variant of a product (e.g., a particular size and color), including its stock.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProductVariant entity.

sku.description: Stock Keeping Unit, a unique identifier for this specific product variant. Must be unique.

color.description: The color of this product variant (e.g., 'Red', 'Blue').

size.description: The size of this product variant (e.g., 'S', 'M', 'L', 'XL').

additionalPrice.description: An additional price adjustment for this variant relative to the base product price. Can be positive or negative.

stockQuantity.description: The current quantity of this product variant available in stock.

imageUrl.description: URL to a variant-specific image, if different from the main product images.

Review.title: Review
Review.description: Captures user feedback and ratings for a specific product.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Review entity.

rating.description: The star rating given by the user (typically 1-5).

comment.description: The textual review provided by the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the review was submitted.

Cart.title: Cart
Cart.description: Represents a user's shopping cart, containing references to individual cart items.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Cart entity.

userId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 Cart). Each user has one active cart.

cartItemIds.description: References to CartItem entities. (Relationship: Cart 1:N CartItem)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the cart was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the cart was last updated.

CartItem.title: CartItem
CartItem.description: Represents a single item (product variant) within a shopping cart.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CartItem entity.

quantity.description: The quantity of the product variant in the cart item.

priceAtAddition.description: The price of the product variant at the time it was added to the cart, including any variant-specific adjustments.

Wishlist.title: Wishlist
Wishlist.description: Stores a list of products that a user is interested in for future purchase.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Wishlist entity.

userId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 Wishlist). Each user has one active wishlist.

productIds.description: References to Product entities in the wishlist. (Relationship: Product N:N Wishlist)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the wishlist was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the wishlist was last updated.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's confirmed order, including details like shipping, billing, and payment status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

billingAddressId.description: Reference to Address for billing. (Relationship: Address 1:N Order)

shippingAddressId.description: Reference to Address for shipping. (Relationship: Address 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: Timestamp when the order was placed.

totalAmount.description: The final total amount of the order after discounts and shipping.

discountAmount.description: The total discount applied to the order, if any.

shippingCost.description: The cost of shipping for this order.

couponCodeId.description: Reference to CouponCode used for this order, if any. (Relationship: CouponCode 1:N Order)

paymentTransactionId.description: Reference to PaymentTransaction for this order. (Relationship: PaymentTransaction 1:1 Order)

orderItemIds.description: References to OrderItem entities. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the order was last updated.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a single item (product variant) included in a confirmed order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

quantity.description: The quantity of the product variant in this order item.

pricePerUnit.description: The price of the product variant at the time the order was placed.

CouponCode.title: CouponCode
CouponCode.description: Defines discount coupons that can be applied to orders.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CouponCode entity.

code.description: The unique code customers use to apply the coupon (e.g., 'SAVE10'). Must be unique.

description.description: A description of the coupon's offer.

discountValue.description: The value of the discount (e.g., 10 for 10% or $10 fixed).

minimumOrderAmount.description: The minimum order total required to apply this coupon.

usageLimit.description: The maximum number of times this coupon can be used across all customers.

timesUsed.description: The current number of times this coupon has been used.

validFrom.description: The date and time from which the coupon is valid.

validUntil.description: The date and time until which the coupon is valid.

isActive.description: Indicates if the coupon is currently active and usable.

PaymentTransaction.title: PaymentTransaction
PaymentTransaction.description: Records details of a payment transaction for an order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PaymentTransaction entity.

transactionId.description: The transaction ID provided by the payment gateway.

amount.description: The total amount of the transaction.

webhookReceived.description: Indicates if the payment gateway's webhook for this transaction was successfully received and processed.

OTPRequest.title: OTPRequest
OTPRequest.description: Stores temporary One-Time Passwords (OTP) sent for user authentication.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OTPRequest entity.

identifier.description: The email address or phone number to which the OTP was sent.

type.description: The type of identifier used for the OTP ('email' or 'phone').

isUsed.description: Indicates if the OTP has already been successfully used for authentication.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the OTP was generated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is path-based for users (`request.auth.uid == userId`). The `role` field within the document serves as the source of truth for user roles.
Stores physical addresses belonging to a specific user. Access is path-based, ensuring only the owning user can manage their addresses.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the address.

params.name: addressId
params.description: The unique identifier of the address document.

definition.description: Stores the single active shopping cart for a user. Access is path-based, allowing only the owning user to manage their cart.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the cart.

definition.description: Stores individual items within a user's shopping cart. Access is path-based, inheriting authorization from the parent user's cart.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the cart item.

params.name: cartItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the cart item.

definition.description: Stores the single active wishlist for a user. Access is path-based, allowing only the owning user to manage their wishlist.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the wishlist.

definition.description: Stores confirmed orders placed by a specific user. Access is path-based, ensuring only the owning user can view/manage their orders. Admins can view/manage all orders.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who placed the order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the order.

definition.description: Stores individual items within a specific user's order. Access is path-based, inheriting authorization from the parent order.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the order item.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the order item.

definition.description: Stores the payment transaction details for a specific user's order. Access is path-based, inheriting authorization from the parent order.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user associated with the payment transaction.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

definition.description: Stores product categories. Publicly readable for all users. Admin-only write access for management.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product category.

definition.description: Stores product catalog items. Publicly readable for all users. Admin-only write access for management.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product.

definition.description: Stores specific variants of a product. Publicly readable as part of product details. Admin-only write access for management.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent product.

params.name: variantId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product variant.

definition.description: Stores user reviews for products. Publicly readable for all users. Users can write/update/delete their own review; includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence. Admin can manage all reviews.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier of the product being reviewed.

params.name: reviewId
params.description: The unique identifier for the review.

definition.description: Stores discount coupon codes. Publicly readable for validation during checkout. Admin-only write access for management.

params.name: couponCodeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the coupon code.

definition.description: Stores One-Time Passwords for authentication. Strictly backend-managed; no client access allowed. Used by trusted Cloud Functions for OTP verification.

params.name: otpRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier for the OTP request.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for efficient Firebase Security Rules to check if a user has administrative privileges ('Existence over Content' pattern). Populated and managed by a trusted backend service based on the `UserProfile.role` field.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who holds an administrative role.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for NexGenShop is designed with a strong emphasis on security, scalability, and debuggability, adhering strictly to the provided core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
This design prioritizes Authorization Independence by eliminating the need for `get()` calls within security rules for authorization logic. 
1.  **Path-Based Ownership:** For user-specific data (e.g., `Address`, `Cart`, `Wishlist`, `Order`, `CartItem`, `OrderItem`, `PaymentTransaction`), data is nested under `/userProfiles/{userId}/...`. This automatically makes `request.auth.uid == userId` a simple and efficient authorization check, removing any need for denormalization beyond what the path inherently provides. All documents at these paths implicitly inherit the `userId` for authorization context.
2.  **Explicit `userId` in Document:** For shared collections where a user might only modify their own contribution (e.g., `Review`), the `userId` is explicitly denormalized into the `Review` document itself (`/products/{productId}/reviews/{reviewId}` will contain a `userId` field). This allows rules to evaluate `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` without needing to `get()` the user's profile or a parent document.
3.  **Dedicated Admin Role Collection:** To avoid `get()` calls for global role checks, a separate `/roles_admin/{userId}` collection is introduced. The *existence* of a document at `/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)` indicates administrative privilege. This is an 'existence over content' pattern for DBAC, making admin role checks atomic and independent.

**QAPs (Queries are not Filters):**
The structure supports secure `list` operations (QAPs) by ensuring that query results are naturally constrained by security rules, rather than relying on rules to filter results after they've been fetched (which is inefficient and insecure).
1.  **Structural Segregation:** Collections are segregated based on their security posture. Publicly accessible data (e.g., `Category`, `Product`, `ProductVariant`, `CouponCode`) resides in top-level collections, allowing full read access. User-specific data is always nested under `userProfiles/{userId}`, meaning a user can only `list` documents within their own `/userProfiles/{userId}/...` subtree, inherently enforcing ownership without filtering.
2.  **Denormalized Authorization:** In the `reviews` collection, which is publicly readable for listing all reviews for a product, the `userId` is denormalized within each review document. This allows a user to *write*, *update*, or *delete* only their *own* review, while still allowing everyone to *read* all reviews for a product without extra filtering. This separation of read vs. write authorization on shared resources is robust.

**DBAC and Data Clarity:**
Roles are stored in `UserProfile.role` (source of truth), but for rule efficiency, the `/roles_admin/{userId}` collection is used for quick authorization checks. Entity names are consistently derived from schemas, wildcards are descriptive, and clear hierarchical paths are used for user-owned data, enhancing debuggability and predictability.
